blog.seoTitle: Mindorion Blog — Guides and professional advice
blog.seoDescription: Mindorion articles on document quality, compliance, B2B outreach and operational best practices.
blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Short, useful articles to reinforce operational quality before every external interaction.

hiddenRisks.title: The hidden risks inside your Word documents
hiddenRisks.excerpt: Comments, metadata and history can reveal far more than expected in a file sent to a client.

metadata.heading: Metadata tells the story behind the scenes
metadata.body: Real author, edit history, internal filenames or previous versions can all create noise around your professional message. Mature teams build a systematic control before any external send.

comments.heading: Comments and annotations remain one of the most common misses
comments.body: In fast editing cycles, internal remarks sometimes survive into the final version. The risk is not only awkwardness: it can expose pricing logic, critiques or sensitive discussions.

workflow.heading: Create a final checkpoint in the workflow
workflow.body: The right practice is not to rely on memory, but to industrialize the last check. A document quality gate reduces variance, reassures teams and protects the commercial relationship.

consultingQualityGate.title: Qualion: a quality gate for consultants
consultingQualityGate.excerpt: For a consultancy or freelancer, a visible mistake in a document can cost more than a few minutes: it can weaken trust.

trust.heading: Trust is built in the details
trust.body: Clients read a document as a signal of method, rigor and reliability. A typo, comment or track change can challenge the perceived level of professionalism behind the work.

operations.heading: A simple quality gate lifts the whole operation
operations.body: Consultants save time, standardize quality and reduce the mental load of last-minute checks. That keeps attention on business value rather than avoidable risk.

rollout.heading: Deploy it without slowing production
rollout.body: A good quality gate should live at the key moment: right before send. It should not create heavy validation, but provide a fast and clear safety net.

whatGetsCleaned.title: What Qualion cleans inside your files
whatGetsCleaned.excerpt: Cleaning a document is not just about spelling. It is mainly about removing the invisible signals that expose your team.

signal.heading: Remove weak signals that still cost a lot
signal.body: Details that look minor can still damage an offer or a file. Cleaning aims to remove the internal traces that distract the recipient and weaken perception.

personalData.heading: Identify sensitive elements
personalData.body: Names, contact details, comments, personal information or confidential content require specific attention. Automated checks help reduce repetitive omissions.

finalExport.heading: Prepare a clean send-ready version
finalExport.body: The final objective is a coherent, readable document aligned with your standards. The exported version should be ready to leave without new risky manipulation.

roiCleanup.title: The ROI of document cleanup
roiCleanup.excerpt: Return on investment is not limited to time saved. It also includes lower risk, less rework and fewer commercial frictions.

rework.heading: Less downstream rework
rework.body: Every flawed send often creates corrections, a new version and unnecessary explanations. Reducing those loops protects valuable team time.

risk.heading: Avoided risk has real value
risk.body: A weak proposal, a poorly cleaned contract or an incomplete candidate file can cost image, time or compliance exposure. ROI also lives in the incidents that stop happening.

metrics.heading: Measure the right indicators
metrics.body: Correction time, number of incidents, time-to-send and internal satisfaction all help quantify the impact of a standardized document control layer.

removeMetadata.title: How to remove metadata before sending
removeMetadata.excerpt: Word document metadata can expose invisible information at first glance. It is better to build a simple, repeatable method.

sources.heading: Understand where metadata comes from
sources.body: Creation history, author, previous titles and document properties build up naturally as files are edited and moved across systems.

controls.heading: Put the right controls in place
controls.body: A human checklist helps but stays fragile. An automated control at the end of the workflow is far more reliable for checking document properties before send.

governance.heading: Turn the practice into a team standard
governance.body: The real difference comes when metadata removal becomes a shared rule embedded in production and document validation routines.

trackChanges.title: Track changes inside proposals
trackChanges.excerpt: In a commercial proposal, tracked changes can expose your internal trade-offs and blur the message shown to the client.

exposure.heading: Tracked changes reveal internal negotiation
exposure.body: Pricing, margins, reservations or multiple rewrites can appear at the wrong moment. That creates an unfavorable imbalance in the commercial exchange.

prevention.heading: Prevent instead of fixing in a rush
prevention.body: A final control removes the need for urgent correction after the fact. It secures the send while making final-version prep smoother for pre-sales teams.

teamHabits.heading: Build stronger team habits
teamHabits.body: When teams know a quality gate exists, they collaborate with more confidence and normalize offer preparation much faster.

about.seoDescription: Discover Mindorion's mission, team and values for protecting the professional reputation of B2B teams.
about.title: Our mission: protect professional reputation
about.missionText: Mindorion helps B2B teams send stronger documents, offers and messages. We believe perceived quality is a strategic lever, not a cosmetic detail.

team.name: Claire Morel
team.bio: A former consultant who saw how a small document detail could weaken an entire client relationship.

team.name: Julien Armand
team.bio: He designs focused workflows that help teams gain rigor without losing speed.

team.name: Mélanie Roussel
team.bio: She supports B2B organizations adopting better habits in sales and document workflows.

values.title: Clarity
values.text: Readable products, simple signals and fast decisions.

values.title: High standards
values.text: Every detail matters once it shapes the image of a team or brand.

values.title: Pragmatism
values.text: We favor useful workflows that are easy to integrate and immediately actionable.

about.locationText: Mindorion is an Estonian company (OÜ) with a team operating mainly from France. This setup helps us support European teams with strong product and operational culture.

contact.seoDescription: Talk to the Mindorion team about document quality, B2B outreach or operational governance needs.
contact.title: Let's talk about your needs
contact.subtitle: Share your context and we'll come back with a clear and relevant answer.
contact.successText: Thanks, your request has been received.

privacy.seoDescription: Read the Mindorion privacy policy: collected data, usage, retention and GDPR rights.
privacy.title: Privacy

sections.title: Collected data
sections.body: We only collect the information needed to run the marketing site, handle contact requests and improve user experience. This may include form data, aggregated browsing events and limited technical information.

sections.title: Data usage
sections.body: This data is used to answer requests, improve content, measure interest in our offers and maintain site security. It is never resold.

sections.title: Retention
sections.body: Data is kept only for the time strictly necessary for the stated purposes, according to internal retention-limitation and minimization policies.

sections.title: GDPR rights
sections.body: You have rights of access, rectification, deletion, objection and restriction. You can exercise those rights by contacting us by email.

sections.title: DPO contact
sections.body: For any privacy question or data-rights request, contact us at contact@mindorion.com.

terms.seoDescription: Read the terms of use for the Mindorion marketing website.
terms.title: Terms of use

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: These terms govern the use of the Mindorion marketing site and access to its informational content. They clarify the responsibilities of both the publisher and the user.

sections.title: Site access
sections.body: The site is available continuously except during maintenance, technical incidents or force majeure. Mindorion aims for reasonable availability but does not guarantee zero interruption.

sections.title: Intellectual property
sections.body: Content, marks, text, interfaces and visual elements on the site are protected. Any unauthorized reproduction is prohibited.

sections.title: Responsibilities
sections.body: Users remain responsible for how they use the information available on the site. Mindorion cannot be held liable for an improper interpretation or use of published content.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: For any request regarding these terms, you can write to contact@mindorion.com.

security.seoDescription: Mindorion commitments on EU hosting, encryption, GDPR and zero unnecessary data retention.
security.title: Security & Compliance
security.subtitle: Clear principles to limit exposure, raise standards and protect B2B document flows.

cards.title: EU hosting
cards.text: Infrastructure designed to support European localization and governance expectations.

cards.title: Encryption
cards.text: Exchanges and processing are protected with security mechanisms suited to professional workflows.

cards.title: GDPR
cards.text: The product encourages data minimization and stronger operational habits around sensitive documents.

cards.title: No retention
cards.text: The platform is designed to avoid unnecessary data retention beyond operational needs.
